event: Christ Served by Angels after the Temptation
symbols: angels serving food — divine provision after the trial of temptation
fruit offered on a platter — the sustenance God provides to those who resist the devil
cherubim with crown of flowers — heavenly honor bestowed on the victorious Christ
wilderness setting — the place of spiritual testing now transformed into a place of comfort
Christ seated in repose — rest and restoration after the ordeal of temptation
description: Christ sits at the left beneath a rocky overhang, wearing a blue and pink robe, with a contemplative or weary expression. A winged angel in a golden and pink garment kneels before him, offering a platter of fruit. A small putto at the lower left also offers food. Above in the sky, two cherubim fly carrying a garland or crown of flowers. The setting is a rocky wilderness with a patch of blue sky visible beyond the rock formation.
theological_description: Bocanegra's painting depicts the moment after Christ's forty-day fast and temptation in the wilderness. Matthew records: 'Then the devil left him, and behold, angels came and were ministering to him' (Matthew 4:11). After resisting three temptations from Satan — to turn stones to bread, to test God by jumping from the temple, and to worship the devil for worldly power — Jesus was served by angels. This scene is theologically rich. Christ had rejected Satan's offer to turn stones to bread, declaring 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God' (Matthew 4:4, quoting Deuteronomy 8:3). Now the Father provides food through angels, vindicating Christ's trust. The scene fulfills the promise of Psalm 91:11-12, which Satan had misquoted during the temptation: 'He will command his angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways.' James later distills the principle: 'Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you' (James 4:7-8). The cherubim with the crown echo the promise: 'Blessed is the man who remains steadfast under trial, for when he has stood the test he will receive the crown of life' (James 1:12).

scripture_refs.reason: Angels coming and ministering to Jesus after the devil departed — the direct scene depicted
The three temptations Christ resisted, after which this angelic ministry occurs
Resist the devil and he will flee — Christ's victory over temptation rewarded by divine comfort
The crown of life for those who endure testing — the cherubim carrying a crown above Christ
God commanding angels concerning the faithful — fulfilled after the temptation Satan misused this psalm
